changes.reason: Batch A isolated call-wall sample build. The current one trade was exceptionally clean, but the 1B net DEX floor likely under-samples the regime; 750M is the next looser grid point while keeping real dealer-flow confirmation.
Lower from 0.05 to 0.03 to admit moderate forward-DEX acceptance states without dropping the forward-flow concept entirely.
Current 0.10 expansion may be too restrictive for a profile with only one trade. Testing 0.05 builds sample while still requiring expanding call volume.
Pair the looser DEX and call-volume thresholds with longer strike acceptance. Moving to 30 seconds should reduce one-print call-wall chop risk versus simply loosening all admission gates.

recommendation.expected_effect: Should increase call_wall_acceptance_long trade count from the current single clean VALID_0DTE sample while preserving option-confirmed admission quality. Success requires at least 3 profile trades, positive standalone PnL, PnL/exposure near or above 250, no slow >=12h trades, and total net profit within 98% of the leader/base. Main tradeoff is possible cannibalization of stronger pullback or charm entries if moderate DEX states are lower quality.
recommendation.label: promote
recommendation.rationale: Choose Batch A because the current leader/base is already strong, broad veto and direct 1DTE tightening have poor protected-winner tradeoffs, and call_wall_acceptance_long is under-sampled but clean: 1 VALID_0DTE trade, $91 PnL, $455/hour, worst MAE only -11.7, high setup quality, and ADX 42.75. This is one logical family and does not touch missing/stale 0DTE routing or global entry vetoes.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: call_wall_acceptance_long has only one trade but produced $91, $455 PnL/exposure hour, worst MAE -11.7, median setup quality 72.5, and high ADX. That suggests the profile may be too tightly sampled rather than unprofitable.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Call Wall Acceptance Sample Builder
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Looser DEX and call-volume thresholds may admit call-wall chop or cannibalize better pullback/charm entries without adding net PnL.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: VALID_0DTE call-wall acceptance with sustained strike acceptance, positive forward DEX, and expanding call volume.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: At least 3 call_wall_acceptance_long trades, positive profile PnL, profile PnL/exposure >=250 preferred, worst MAE <= -150, total net profit >=98% of leader, and slow_12h remains 0.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: charm_pin_pullback_long contributes $1200.3 across 32 trades with fast median hold, but owns the global -513.4 MAE. Static broad filters overlap protected extended-VWAP winners, so path-aware separation remains the cleaner design.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Can overfit delayed winners or block clean high-extension scalps unless protected fast-winner retention is explicitly validated.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long high-extension entries that fail to make early positive progress before MAE.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL, >=95% protected fast winners, total net profit >=98% of leader, and no slow trades.
